questions.question: The force which produces an acceleration equal to the acceleration due to gravity in unit mass of is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to Newton's second law of motion.

questions.question: The rate of change of momentum of an object is proportional to the _______ applied on an object.
questions.explanation: This concept is related to Newton's second law of motion.

questions.question: The component of contact force or reaction perpendicular to the plane of contact is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to friction and normal forces.

questions.question: The tendency of a body to continue in its state of rest, even when some external force is applied, is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to Newton's first law of motion.

questions.question: The force which opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact with each other is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to friction and normal forces.

questions.question: The property of a body due to which it tends to resist any change in its state of rest or motion is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to Newton's first law of motion.

questions.question: The force that acts on an object due to the presence of a magnetic field around it is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to magnetism and electromagnetism.

questions.question: The rate at which work is done by a force on an object is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to work, energy, and power.

questions.question: The force that acts on an object due to the presence of an electric field around it is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to electricity and electromagnetism.

questions.question: The rate at which energy is transferred from one object to another due to a force acting on it is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to work, energy, and power.

questions.question: The force that acts on an object due to the presence of a gravitational field around it is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to gravity and Newton's law of universal gravitation.

questions.question: The property of a body due to which it tends to resist any change in its state of motion or rest is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to Newton's first law of motion.

questions.question: The force that acts on an object due to the presence of a magnetic field around it, causing it to change its direction or speed, is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to magnetism and electromagnetism.

questions.question: The rate at which work is done by a force on an object, causing it to change its state of motion or rest, is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to work, energy, and power.

questions.question: The force that acts on an object due to the presence of an electric field around it, causing it to change its direction or speed, is called _______
questions.explanation: This concept is related to electricity and electromagnetism.
